comment.body: While others are sharing perspective, as a subscriber since I believe February paying varying degrees of attention, there appears to have been a drastic turn away from tangible data towards 'macro' driving the boat -- 'macro' typically being a codeword, universally, for one's biases of reading the world. References to underground geopolitical allegiances, codes held within portraits, political policies which will exist for 'x number of years or more' .. the technical readings & levels remain immensely valuable, but trading along these other pathways would seem to be a disaster in the making. Simply my opinion of course ..

comment.body: I have nothing bad to say about Alma or the service she provides. But I can say that and be skeptical. I don’t know if she really has any insider connections…maybe she just follows the right X accounts. Who knows if she’s a long term profitable trader? Who knows if she’s actually a female? She’s anon and has a certain persona. It is weird to me her writing style is different across different mediums. But I enjoy her insight, indicators, signals, etc. But I’m certainly not thinking she has some ridiculous Jim Simon’s type edge

comment.body: Coming from a place of concern then that’s fair enough. I still don’t see what Alma should do about it, she literally made educational chat posts about risk management 101. But those same people jumping in head long with bad risk management into trades are likely the same ones who will not take the time to read those posts or the weekly and daily posts. They probably wouldn’t read or listen to warnings either, sometimes the pain of loss is the only way to learn.

comment.body: Currently the risks haven’t been ruled out, and actually the whole situation doesn’t look good. In this environment, it makes sense to be hedged and to build “lifeboats”. And if my hedges don’t print, it would probably mean that I won’t need my real lifeboat, so in either case I will have something good.
